item.title: backlog add interactive mode — guided item creation via prompts
item.intent: Users must be able to create a complete, sprint-ready backlog item interactively without knowing all the flags — interactive mode closes the discovery gap for new users.
item.description: backlog add currently requires all fields to be passed as flags (--title, --intent, --priority, etc.) or creates a minimal item with empty fields. New users often don't know what fields are needed for sprint readiness. An interactive mode (triggered when no flags are passed, or with --interactive) should prompt for: type (feat/bug), title, intent, priority, steps (repeatable until empty), and acceptance criteria (repeatable until empty). Each prompt should show the field's purpose and an example. At the end, show the item and ask to confirm before writing.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_backlog_add() and detect when no flags were passed (or --interactive was given)
Enter interactive mode: prompt for type with valid options shown; prompt for title; prompt for intent with hint about directive format; prompt for priority (must/should/could/future);
Prompt for steps in a loop: 'Step N (empty to finish):' — accumulate non-empty responses into steps array
Prompt for AC in a loop: 'AC N (empty to finish):' — accumulate non-empty responses into AC array
Display a summary of the item to be created and ask 'Create this item? (Y/n)' — if confirmed, create the item; if declined, exit without writing
Ensure interactive mode is skipped when stdin is not a terminal (piped usage) — check with [[ -t 0 ]]
acceptanceCriteria: backlog add with no flags prompts interactively for title, intent, priority, steps, and AC
User can press Enter on a step or AC prompt to stop adding entries (empty input ends the loop)
Interactive mode is skipped when stdin is not a tty (piped usage falls through to flag-based path)
The created item passes backlog check (all required fields populated)
